task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the 'DevOps Automation' project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenProject interface shows 'DevOps Automation' as the active project in the top header or sidebar.

task_completion.description: The agent opened the Members page for the project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Members' tab or page is active within the 'DevOps Automation' project menu.

task_completion.description: The agent selected 'Carol Williams' (carol.williams) to be added.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Add member' modal or form shows 'Carol Williams' or 'carol.williams' selected in the user search or input field.

task_completion.description: The agent selected the 'Developer' role for the user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The role dropdown or checkbox for 'Developer' is selected in the 'Add member' form.

task_completion.description: The user 'Carol Williams' is successfully added to the project with the 'Developer' role.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Members list for 'DevOps Automation' displays 'Carol Williams' with the role 'Developer', or a green success toast/notification confirms the addition.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenProject web interface rather than terminal commands or API calls.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser interacting with the OpenProject UI, not a terminal window executing curl or rails console commands.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenProject application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows localhost:8080 and the standard OpenProject interface is visible, rather than a mock HTML file or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a database client or terminal to directly insert the user-project relation.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows open with psql, mysql, or sqlite prompts modifying the database.
